title: adenostemma parviflorum
intro: Adenostemma parviflorum, also known as "Bahasa" or "Pride of India," is a flowering plant species in the family Acanthaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. It has been utilized for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Adenostemma parviflorum include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and reduce oxidative stress. In traditional medicine, Adenostemma parviflorum is often prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
